title: Special mechanic review
principles: A supplied special mechanic may refine the weapon's role, but every conclusion must be traceable to the explicit mechanic text.
Describe the mechanic directly without converting it into an unsupported statistic or improvement parameter.
Do not recommend changing the special mechanic itself; recommend only allowed statistics that reinforce its demonstrated function or correct supported friction.
Distinguish damage ramp from charge behavior, chaining from radial damage, and secondary targets from the primary target.
When a mechanic includes target count, range, decay, duration, or activation conditions, preserve those distinctions.
If the mechanic is ambiguous, prefer a cautious description and avoid deterministic claims.
exceptions: Do not infer mechanics from the weapon name.
Do not assume every special mechanic creates multi-target or area behavior.
Do not invent hidden scaling, activation conditions, or external synergies.
